reasoning: Stub critic (autonomous run, 2026-05-31): Phase C Task C4 records ADR-067 — the snapshotter (@chiefaia/design-ingest) keeps row-level tenant_id through V1; schema-level convergence is explicitly deferred to a future ADR triggered by 10M row count OR 25 tenant count, whichever fires first. Operator directive 'decide for me, optimize for what reduces drift' is resolved on the read 'reduces drift' = 'smallest change that keeps V1 shippable'; the migration itself is the larger risk than the drift it would remove. Drift becomes intentional and dated rather than accidental. The ADR follows the caia-ea Nygard template, lives canonically at caia-ea/decisions/ADR-067-...md, and is mirrored verbatim at caia/docs/adr/ADR-067-...md with a top-line callout to the canonical source. The mirror is the greppable copy that lives next to the code it constrains; the cross-citation (in both directions) prevents silent drift between the two files. 6 vitest cases (7 assertions) cover existence, Accepted status, affected-component naming, the decision sentence, both migration triggers, and the cross-citation. No runtime code change. Subscription-only LLM discipline: this PR adds no LLM calls. Operator should run live submitPlan against @caia/ea-architect before True-Zero admin-merge.
requested_modifications: Operator: run live submitPlan against @caia/ea-architect before True-Zero admin-merge.
